findings: Mamba is a selective state-space architecture: SSM parameters (Δ, B, C) are functions of input, breaking time-invariance and enabling content-based selection
Linear scaling in sequence length vs Transformer quadratic; hardware-aware scan algorithm avoids materializing the expanded state, runs up to 3× faster than prior SSM on A100
5× higher inference throughput than Transformers of similar size due to constant-time-per-step autoregressive unrolling (no KV cache)

Mamba-3B matches Transformer-7B quality on common-sense reasoning (e.g. 4 points higher avg vs Pythia-3B and exceeds Pythia-7B); first linear-time model to truly match Transformer pretraining perplexity
Performance keeps improving with longer context up to 1M-token sequences on real data
Solves synthetic tasks like selective copy and induction heads that prior SSM variants fail; can extrapolate to >1M tokens
limitations: Pure Mamba shows weaker in-context retrieval than attention (later Jamba paper confirms); evaluated up to 1B params at the time of release — scaling-law extrapolations beyond not yet tested in this paper.
relevance: Core comparative claim. Direct head-to-head Mamba vs Transformer on language modeling at matched parameter scale.
